summary: The iteration-3 design fully covers business requirements R1-R18, and the two iteration-3 changes (F1 tone-source unification, F2 test-strategy note) are guide-alignment refinements that do not alter requirement coverage. An independent trace maps every requirement to a concrete component: surface placement and load-time truth (R1, R2, R15) via the two-variant _status_summary partial plus thin controllers using .with_board; the plain-language state machine (R3-R13) via Pipelines::StatusSummary's ordered resolution, including first-attempt iteration suppression (R4), two-step 'name both' (R5), three-plus 'N steps are running' collapse (R6), awaiting-human-and-where (R7), completed (R8), a distinct failed branch that names the phase/step where it stopped (R9), not-started (R10), aborted->'Canceled' with a future paused->'Paused' mapping (R11), and an unconditional catch-all that makes build a total function so the summary is never blank or wrong (R12); everyday wording with no codes/IDs (R13); live push within seconds (R14) via Pipelines::BroadcastStatus fired from the three run transitions and the essential ManagerTick seam; ordering convergence by re-deriving from fresh DB state on each broadcast (R16); and accessibility with a status dot always paired with the summary words plus one shared derivation for both surfaces (R17, R18). Nothing is designed that no requirement asks for: the sole branch beyond the R-list (Blocked/stuck) is a real reachable pipeline state given a truthful sentence explicitly justified under R12, and the previously-flagged out-of-scope 'as_of' timestamp was removed. No requirement is unaddressed and no unrequested behavior is introduced.
